system_prompt: You are a Minecraft reconstruction planner. Return only one strict JSON object with a compact build plan.
prompt:
You are given a textual building description.
Create an executable build plan using ONLY these operation types:
- fill: axis-aligned cuboid fill
- carve: axis-aligned cuboid set to air
- set: single block placement

Output STRICT JSON with this schema:
{
  "plan_version": "v1",
  "bbox": {"xmin": 0, "xmax": 0, "ymin": 0, "ymax": 0, "zmin": 0, "zmax": 0},
  "palette": {
    "wall": "stonebrick",
    "roof": "wood",
    "trim": "brick",
    "glass": "glass",
    "light": "glowstone",
    "floor": "wood"
  },
  "operations": [
    {"op": "fill", "x1": 0, "y1": 0, "z1": 0, "x2": 1, "y2": 1, "z2": 1, "block": "stonebrick", "purpose": "base"},
    {"op": "carve", "x1": 0, "y1": 0, "z1": 0, "x2": 0, "y2": 0, "z2": 0, "purpose": "hollow"},
    {"op": "set", "x": 0, "y": 0, "z": 0, "block": "glowstone", "purpose": "light"}
  ],
  "notes": ["optional"]
}

Rules:
- Coordinates must be integers.
- Keep bbox anchored near origin and positive when possible.
- operations count <= 260.
- Create a complete, coherent building: floor, walls, roof, windows, entrance.
- Use normalized material IDs when possible.

Description JSON:
{
  "summary": "A long rectangular, two-sided building with a hollow central courtyard (u-shaped in plan) composed of layered horizontal material bands, raised stone/brick base, vertical white corner/column features, and steep dark red gabled roof ridges running along the long sides. The structure appears symmetrical along its central long axis and is a single main floor with a raised parapet/roof structure above.",
  "building_type": "industrial/warehouse-style facade (decorative shell)",
  "shape": {
    "overall_form": "U-shaped ring forming two long parallel wings connected at one short end, creating an open courtyard on one short side",
    "footprint_hint": "u_shape",
    "symmetry": "bilaterally symmetrical along the central long axis (mirror left-right)",
    "floors_estimate": 1,
    "roof_type": "gable"
  },
  "dimensions_estimate": {
    "width": 21,
    "depth": 11,
    "height": 8
  },
  "materials": [
    {
      "name": "brick",
      "role": "mid-wall band",
      "confidence": 0.9
    },
    {
      "name": "stonebrick",
      "role": "foundation/lower course",
      "confidence": 0.7
    },
    {
      "name": "quartz",
      "role": "white vertical columns and horizontal trim",
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "name": "nether_brick",
      "role": "roof ridge and sloped roof",
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "name": "wood",
      "role": "thin horizontal band (planks) between brick bands",
      "confidence": 0.8
    },
    {
      "name": "slab_stone",
      "role": "parapet/edge trim",
      "confidence": 0.6
    },
    {
      "name": "glowstone",
      "role": "single light at entrance (possible interior)",
      "confidence": 0.5
    }
  ],
  "elements": [
    "vertical quartz corner/column posts at regular intervals",
    "three horizontal material bands across wall (stonebrick base, brick middle, wood plank strip)",
    "raised nether_brick gabled roof ridges along each long side with stepped end crenellations",
    "open central courtyard (ground level dirt shown)",
    "perimeter low quartz slab/border blocks on ground around building"
  ],
  "rebuild_hints": [
    "Construct two long parallel walls approximately 21x8 (WxH) blocks and leave the short end between them open to form a U shape; connect one short end with the same wall composition.",
    "Layer walls from bottom: stonebrick base ~1-2 blocks high, then a brick band ~2 blocks high, a wood plank stripe ~1 block, repeat brick, then quartz horizontal trim and vertical quartz columns placed every ~6 blocks along long walls.",
    "Place nether_brick stairs/blocks to form steep gable roof ridges centered on each long wall; add quartz slab/blocks as a thin parapet under the roof edge and stepped nether_brick crenellations at the gable ends.",
    "Add a row of quartz slabs/blocks around the outside ground edge as a decorative border and leave interior courtyard ground as dirt.",
    "Include single small entrance detail centered on connected short end with glowstone behind an opening (uncertain)."
  ],
  "uncertainties": [
    "Exact block types for white trim likely quartz but could be smooth_sandstone or another white block; quartz confidence high but not certain.",
    "Exact depth and internal structure are unknown; interior appears empty and only exterior shell is visible.",
    "Dimensions are approximate by block count from images and may be off by a few blocks per axis.",
    "The thin gray band under the white trim could be stone_slab or smooth stone; identified as slab_stone with moderate confidence.",
    "Lighting and small decorative details (e.g., glowstone at entrance) are partially obscured and uncertain."
  ],
  "provider": "openai",
  "model": "gpt-5-mini",
  "building": "building_000",
  "created_at": "2026-02-28T15:03:14.921587+00:00"
}
